raw_payload.author: solinsbiosalong
raw_payload.content:
I feel violated and heartbroken.

How could u slide in a gilmore girl spoiler and think u could just get away with it? I'm still watching the freaking show. U wanted to attract younger audiences, but this was rly not the way to go.

The only scene that caught my attention was this one. But wth is a movie if it needs a gilmore girl spoiler for publicity?

This movie has so much wasted potential and that scene was my last straw. I turned the tv off.

But hey, what do I know? Maybe I'm just rly bitter.

raw_payload.author: danieljfarthing
raw_payload.content: Daft comic shoot-em-up "Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice" is set over the party night Jimmy Tatro's released from prison and his dad Keith David (their unnamed city's top mobster) puts a hit on the rat who put him there. That pulls two henchmen, James Marsden & Vince Vaughan (and the latter's wife Eiza González), into a twisty caper involving Ben Schwartz's time-machine (!), a second Vaughan, a bangin' soundtrack, and minor characters like Stephen Root, Dolph Lundgren, & Emily Hampshire. Writer / director BenDavid Grabinski's second film won't win awards but it's well made, well performed (esp González, Marsden & Tatro), fun, unpretentious, & unashamedly feel-good. Fair play. Crackin'.

raw_payload.author: dcj2
raw_payload.content:
Really wanted to like this, but boy, what a let down. I've enjoyed Vince's patented, fast-talking wise-cracking persona in virtually every other movie he's made, so I was intrigued with his more subtle, sinister start to this. Sadly that was about the peak of my interest. This rapidly devoles into a silly wannabe action flick with some sci-fi touches. Ultimately, the action scenes just aren't that exciting, the silly bits aren't funny (but I will confess that Steven Root's brief scene did give me a chuckle) the dialog is forced and cringe worthy. The film attempts to be irreverent at times, but again, those attempts sent my cringe-meter off the scale. One example, midway thru we're treated to a scene with the three (four?) leads discussing "Gilmore Girls"??? Seriously? It just takes the viewer's out fragile bubble of interest and shatters it completely.

I wish the cast had been given better material to work with, it had a lot of potential.

raw_payload.author: pzrqy
raw_payload.content: I wanted an easy and fun watch, and this one seems like it would fit the bills. I tried this - and I gave up halfway through. Doesn't live up the expectation and I got bored. It is not exciting enough to keep the viewer entertained. It is a shame though as they have good actors. Don't know whether the ending would be better.

raw_payload.author: wckedmnkey-828-307083
raw_payload.content: This is and enjoyable, lighthearted sci-fi comedy that prioritizes chemistry and pacing over individual virtuosity. Part of me wishes they would have let Vaughn loose with his over the top verbal diarea (which i quite enjoy), But they made a solid choice, keeping him slightly on a leash to allow his charachter to be the heart at the center of the film.

raw_payload.author: roncarnes95
raw_payload.content: This film is about a 6.2. It's an entertaining made-for-streaming level script. But the juddery slow motion made me feel like I was watching a movie directed by a C-grade John Wu imitator. The use of Steve Winwood's "Valerie" during a coked up mob after-party was really cringe worthy. Yes, the lyrics are mainly why it was used, but a remix or modern cover would have been really useful. I wish Grabinski had Danny Boyle or Guy Ritchie on speed dial for soundtrack advice. Also, I loved "Block Rockin' Beats" as much as anyone back in the day, but it hasn't aged well. It's always fun seeing James Marsden. Ditto Vince Vaughn when he's in something half-decent, which this movie qualifies as. There are some decent running jokes, and more suspense than you would expect. The whole cast is enjoyable, especially the character actors you know you've seen somewhere before. If this film had a better director, a much better soundtrack, and the ending before the feel-good sequel set-up that followed it, this movie could have shined more. I'm not ashamed to say that the initial ending made me tear up some. M+N+N+A was definitely worth the watch, but some tweaks could have made it a B movie classic.

raw_payload.author: MorfenZ
raw_payload.content:
Mike and Nick and Nick and Ellis (2026) was honestly just a really fun watch for me. I went in not expecting anything deep, and that's exactly why it worked-it knows it's a crime-comedy and just sticks to being entertaining. Coming from someone connected to Tenet and Christopher Nolan, it's funny how different the vibe is-way more relaxed and easy to enjoy.

There were a lot of genuinely funny moments, and I actually liked the action too-it keeps things moving without getting boring. The cast really clicked for me, which made the whole thing even more enjoyable.

Since crime-comedy is one of my favorite genres, this felt like a perfect "just sit back and enjoy" type of movie. It's not something I'd revisit a lot, but as a one-time watch, I had a really good time with it.

raw_payload.author: paulmbyrnes
raw_payload.content: Started out ok. Funny at times and an easy watch. But as movie progressed the jokes got worse and the plot was predictable and boring. Had a good cast but this couldn't save it. Progressively got more and more slapstick. Basically if they pulled a 'yikes!' sign Warner bros style I wouldn't be surprised at all. We ended up just walking away and started cleaning the house. Didn't even care how it ended.

raw_payload.author: GarrettG-50
raw_payload.content: The only thing better than Vince Vaughn in a movie is two Vince Vaughn in a movie. It just makes it right. It was a clever original movie. It's fun to watch good cast. I enjoyed it. It's not going to win any Oscars. Still a great movie. The Cameo Dolph lundgren I thought was awesome as well. The only benefit to the world post covid and the movies being cranked out is every couple movies or at least decent mixed with about seven or eight terrible ones. But they come out so quick that you get quite a few good movies. This is one of them.

raw_payload.author: kvvinkler
raw_payload.content:
Vince Vaughn proves once again that he's a guarantee for smart, high-quality, genuinely funny comedy. I loved his lead role in Bad Monkey, and just when I was starting to worry that good comedies had vanished, this film blew me away!

Every character shines, even the ones with only 10 minutes on screen - clearly, the cast gave 110%! Fast-paced, dynamic, full of character development, backstories, and even post-stories. And the best part? Zero vulgarity. ZERO. In a modern comedy. Incredible.

Dolph Lundgren's cameo is a perfect cherry on top.

This film is a must-watch for anyone who loves action-comedy with a dash of sci-fi. Huge thanks to everyone who made it happen!

To future viewers: grab your popcorn and enjoy!

P. S. Creators: if you're planning a sequel, don't mess it up - the bar is sky-high!

raw_payload.author: DarklyDreamingFan
raw_payload.content:
Some friendships don't fall apart... they unravel.

Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is a sharp, chaotic, and darkly funny character study about identity, ego, and the fragile dynamics of modern relationships. What starts as a seemingly simple story about four friends quickly spirals into something far more unsettling. A layered exploration of insecurity, performance, and the quiet toxicity that builds over time. It's uncomfortable, unpredictable, and strangely intimate. Proof that sometimes the most dangerous conflicts aren't loud. They're personal.

.

Overview (No Spoilers)

The film follows four interconnected characters (Mike, Nick, Nick, and Alice) whose relationships blur the lines between friendship, rivalry, and emotional dependency.

Set over a series of encounters, conversations, and escalating tensions, the story unfolds through overlapping perspectives, revealing contradictions in how each character sees themselves and each other.

As insecurities surface and dynamics shift, what once felt like harmless banter begins to expose deeper issues.

The film thrives in its ambiguity. Forcing viewers to question who's right, who's wrong, and whether those distinctions even matter.

.

What Worked

1. Complex Character Writing: Each character feels real, flawed, and uncomfortably recognizable.

2. Unpredictable Structure: Perspective shifts add depth and recontextualize earlier scenes.

3. Sharp, Natural Dialogue: Conversations feel raw, messy, and authentic.

4. Dark Humor: Awkward, biting moments balance the heavier themes.

5. Thematic Depth: Explores identity, ego, and social dynamics with precision.

6. Performances: Subtle, nuanced acting carries the emotional weight.

7. Escalating Tension: Small moments build into larger emotional conflicts.

.

What Fell Short

1. Ambiguity May Frustrate: Not all questions are answered clearly.

2. Minimal Plot Structure: Focus is on characters rather than events.

3. Unlikable Characters: Intentional, but may distance some viewers.

4. Dialogue-Heavy: Requires attention and patience.

5. Limited Visual Variety: Mostly contained settings.

6. Emotional Intensity: Can feel draining over time.

7. Subtle Payoffs: No big, dramatic resolution.

.

If You Liked This, You Might Also Enjoy

1. The Social Network: for sharp dialogue and ego-driven conflict

2. Marriage Story: for raw emotional realism

3. Bodies Bodies Bodies: for chaotic friend dynamics

4. Before Midnight: for intense relationship dialogue

5. Frances Ha: for identity and friendship themes

6. Euphoria: for emotional volatility and character focus

7. Aftersun: for subtle, layered storytelling

.

Final Thought

Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is not about what happens. It's about what's revealed. A film that dissects friendship with uncomfortable honesty, showing how easily connection can turn into competition, and closeness into quiet resentment. It's messy, intimate, and deliberately unresolved.

It may not give you clear answers, but it leaves you thinking. About your own relationships, your own identity, and the roles we all play.

⭐ 7.1/10. A sharp, uncomfortable, and deeply human exploration of friendship and self-perception.

raw_payload.author: vo_donnell
raw_payload.content: I love all these actors but it's a bit disappointing. Average story line got a bit lost. Don't like the glitch style filming. Don't know why the Gilmore girls was put in , completely loose anyone who hasn't seen it. I have my husband didn't. Waste of time. Vince has filmed better. Didn't really find it that funny.

raw_payload.author: christianaabyorellana
raw_payload.content: The movie started of interesting, but got ridiculous 30 min into the movie and neither Marsden, Eiza nor Vaughn could've save this disaster of a movie even if they acted their hearts out. It has some pretty bad dialogue, story and doesn't really know what it wants during it's set-up for the final act. I really don't get the other reviewers giving it a 6/7, like is the bar that low nowadays? Is just the presence of liked actors enough to boost the score. I don't know, but it you care for your time, skip this one. You ain't missing anything.

raw_payload.author: ergodlove-49942
raw_payload.content:
I enjoyed it, but it was very much the usual made for streaming movie. I think the pacing could be cleaner, Vaughn was decent enough, Gonzalez is superb in everything, and Marsden has been on fire lately. As much as I liked the main actors in this, it just felt a little lazy with how they tried to pull the story together.

The best part of this whole movie though, was their music choice. Reptile and Blade?! Someone really liked those 90s hits, and who can blame 'em.

raw_payload.author: TheMovieSearch
raw_payload.content:
Mike, Nick, Nick, and Alice is one of those movies that lands somewhere right in the middle. It is not bad, it is not amazing, but it is decent enough to keep your attention if you are willing to go along with its odd little mix of futuristic ideas and grounded reality. I honestly expected it to be a bit more entertaining than it ended up being, especially with a cast like this, but it still has enough charm and personality to make it worth checking out. It just never quite reaches the level where it becomes unforgettable.

A lot of the film's appeal comes from the cast, because the actors are doing a lot of the heavy lifting. Vince Vaughn is definitely one of those actors who can be either a huge win or a total miss depending on the project, and here he lands somewhere in the middle. He is not bad at all, but he is not the standout either. He works well enough for the tone of the movie, and even though I was not fully sold on him going in, I ended up liking him more than I expected. James Marsden, on the other hand, is exactly what you would expect him to be: reliable, charismatic, and effortlessly strong in his scenes. He has that natural screen presence that always helps elevate whatever he is in.

The screenplay itself takes a concept that could have very easily become cheesy or overdone. The whole idea of someone coming back from the future to try to change events is not exactly new territory, and a lot of movies have run that concept into the ground. What helps this one is that it does not fully lean into the obvious route. Instead of drowning in sci-fi gimmicks or trying too hard to explain every detail, it keeps things a little more restrained and lets the story unfold in a way that feels lighter and more character-driven. That ultimately works in the movie's favor because it allows the ending to land in a way that feels a bit more unexpected.

Another reason the movie works better than it probably should is because of the supporting cast. Eiza González brings a lot of confidence and screen presence, and she continues to prove that she can slide into different genres without feeling out of place. Keith David is, as always, fantastic and brings a sense of gravitas to everything he touches. Then you have Jimmy Tatro, who adds a lot of the lighter comedic energy and helps keep the movie from feeling too heavy or self-serious. Together, the cast gives the film a stronger pulse than the premise alone might have been able to provide.

What keeps the film from being great, though, is that it never fully commits to being as bold or as emotionally gripping as it could have been. There are moments where it feels like it is about to really dig deeper into its ideas, but then it pulls back and settles into being simply "fine." That is not necessarily a bad thing, but it does mean the movie feels more like a casual watch than something that will stick with you long after the credits roll.

Overall, Mike, Nick, Nick, and Alice is a fun enough film that ends up being better than it first appears, even if it never fully becomes anything spectacular. It has a solid cast, a familiar but workable concept, and just enough charm to make it enjoyable. It is definitely an acquired taste and probably will not become everybody's favorite, but if you go in with balanced expectations, there is a decent chance you will have a good time with it.

raw_payload.author: RayhanA-908
raw_payload.content: The narrative felt a bit disjointed, but the action sequences were undeniably compelling. Overall, it was a satisfactory film, though not one I'd enthusiastically recommend. The slow-motion scenes really took me out of it, contributing to the overall disjointed feel. A more coherent storyline would have definitely elevated the movie! 🎬🍿

raw_payload.author: jessecip313
raw_payload.content:
This was a good movie but I will assume you won't ever watch it again.

Light Spoiler warning

Synopsis A man has the opportunity to go back in time and try to correct something he feels guilty for doing.

Review Vince Vaughn was good as usual. It was gun to see him as a hit man type character that still has the Vince Vaughn charm and humor. The movie flowed well, the casting was great, and we got some pretty good action. There wasn't anything that made me laugh out loud but definitely had fun with the movie.

Heavy spoilers Why didn't Nick go back in time to when he could have set up the guy who was the actual rat? I would imagine taking down the entire organization will still have some consequences.

Answer: we wouldn't have had the movie then.

raw_payload.author: rroelandskeim
raw_payload.content:
A great fast-paced action comedy with a surprisingly tender heart. We were already looking forward to seeing the movie based on the trailer but enjoyed it even more than we thought we would. And the soundtrack is fantastic! We will definitely be adding this one to the rewatch list.

"Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice" stars Vince Vaughn, James Marsden, Eiza González, Keith David, Jimmy Tatro, Stephen Root, Lewis Tan, Ben Schwartz, Emily Hampshire, and Arturo Castro, and is written and directed by BenDavid Grabinski, produced by Andrew Lazar, p.g.a., and executive produced by Richard Middleton and Vanessa Humphrey. The film is a hilarious, stylized, R-rated action-comedy about two gangsters and the woman they love trying to survive the most dangerous night of their lives. As if that wasn't enough, there's one wild ingredient added to the mix: a time machine.

raw_payload.author: ThurstonHunger
raw_payload.content:
One star for each Vince Vaughan for me. If only there were another five or more versions of him? I get that Vaughan's snappy patter is not for everyone, but if like myself you too are a fan - I hope you caught "Bad Monkey" prior to this.

Also if you are a Gilmore Girls fan, you will likely get a kick out of this.

For me, this film could have used more dialog and character interaction (or peppered in more from bits with Ben Schwarz, Stephen Root and whoever the Spicolli-esque convenience store guy was and others like that).

For me, it could have used less of the extended fist fight/wedding dancing/gunslinging scenes.

Hollywood's love of hitmen leaves me cold, this film actually lampoons them in a sort of weird way. Keith David stood out as a man among a bunch of dumb and dumber boys.

By the end, the body count far exceeds the joke count, and it's a bit of a shame as there feels like there was promise here. Besides the time travel angle, I wonder if Vaughan liked the interesting message of atonement.

Or maybe this movie was made as an apology by a producer/director/philanderer to his trophy soon to be ex-wife who was also an extra in the Gilmour Girls?

raw_payload.author: rylo-20770
raw_payload.content: Absolute awful! I do not like to be cynical or negative but I cannot believe a group of people thought this would be a good idea. Hulu/Disney execs, your business model/takeover acquisitions seems to be doing fantastic lately, but please leave the actual filmmaking to..... the filmmakers. Thank you.

raw_payload.author: tinabugvi
raw_payload.content:
Vince Vaughn's best movie since Wedding Crashers. A long overdue comedy that is actually laugh out loud funny. It is setup perfectly for a sequel. Some really hilarious scenes. Overall I thought it was extremely entertaining. At least 2 scenes I missed half of because I was laughing so hard.

I'll definitely watch it again.

raw_payload.author: G0nn4b3
raw_payload.content:
This film perfectly illustrates the biggest problem when someone takes on both writing and directing: there's no one to rein them in when their ideas go off the rails. Unfortunately, that's exactly what happens here.

The movie is genuinely terrible. The characters are painfully underdeveloped-mere sketches rather than real people-making it impossible to care about anything that happens to them. The plot feels nonsensical and often downright idiotic, lacking any sense of purpose or direction.

What makes matters worse is the excessive and unjustified violence. Scene after scene drags on without adding anything meaningful, as if they exist purely to fill time rather than serve the story. Some moments are not just unnecessary but outright absurd-like the bizarre dialogue about "the best years, Of the dick" or the painfully awkward sequences where the father and son fail to communicate because they don't understand each other's references. Instead of being insightful or emotional, these scenes come across as shallow and contrived.

The fight scenes, in particular, are far too long and repetitive, quickly becoming exhausting rather than engaging.

In the end, this is a film that feels entirely pointless. There's no clear reason for its existence, and that makes the wasted potential all the more frustrating. It's disappointing to see talent squandered on something so poorly executed. I genuinely regret spending time watching it.

raw_payload.author: dylanpatterson1987
raw_payload.content: This was an excellent film. I was surprised because I hadn't heard anything about it. I didn't used to be a fan of Vince Vaughn but he's turned me around with a lot of his current stuff like Freaky, in which he was great. This movie was great. The script was clever and funny and the cast had great chemistry because by the end of the film (semi-spoiler) there's a loss that really felt earned, even if it wasn't a permanent one but you'll have to watch to see what I mean. This isn't Casablanca (not that it's trying to be) but it's enjoyable as hell, which I find is rare in bigger budget comedies lately. You won't regret watching this.

raw_payload.author: JackHodges-13503
raw_payload.content:
Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is a gangster film that feels like a polite conversation, right before someone flips the table.

There's one Mike, two Nicks, and Alice caught in the middle of it all, which sounds simple until it absolutely isn't. Keeping up feels like trying to follow who's paying for a round when everyone's pretending they forgot their wallet.

Vince Vaughn plays it completely straight, and it works. No fast talking, no messing about. Just a man who looks like he knows something you don't and isn't planning to explain it.

The whole film runs on tension. Conversations do the damage here, not bullets. Every line feels like it could be the wrong one, and every silence feels worse. It's less "big mob movie" and more "this is going to go sideways any second." It's sharp as well. The name overlap is not just a gimmick, it feeds into the confusion and mistrust. Nobody feels fully in control, and that's the point.

raw_payload.author: wmwheeler8
raw_payload.content: Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is almost what you think it is. This movie is a fun and entertaining action comedy. The cast of characters that show up are done well and are not overused, cameos included. Yes the premise, is absurd, but most movies are. It's nobody's best film or worst. I definitely did not expect it to have as many funny scenes as it did. Icing on the cake, afterthought movie was over, Hulu suggested Gilmore Girls! Now that was funny!

raw_payload.author: ginocox-206-336968
raw_payload.content:
Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice (2026) is pure entertainment for the action/adventure fan. It should sweep win multiple Oscars, but will probably be ignored, as it is a crowd pleaser and not particularly socially relevant.

Vince Vaughn (Nick) plays a gangster who travels back in time to correct a grave injustice for which he feels responsible. The characters are a bit comic-book-ish with exaggerated characteristics and personalities, but it fits the genre. The plot is satisfyingly complex and multi-layered, and is gradually revealed in a way that keeps the audience guessing. Eliza González (Alice) is ideally cast as the femme fatale in a complex role. Vaughn delivers a nuanced performance, as does James Marsden (Quick Draw Mike). Supporting roles are more nuanced and three-dimensional than lead roles in many recent films. The screenplay was top notch.

The fight scenes are expertly choreographed and executed, with some new twists. Cinematography, special effects and other production values are excellent.

On the negative side, there are quite a few popular-culture references, particularly to Gilmore Girls, which seem confusing to one who wasn't even aware that such a series ever existed, although that may have been the comedic intent. While the violence was graphic, the sexuality was soft-pedaled, with strippers and hookers unrealistically modest.

raw_payload.author: roshantj
raw_payload.content: It's not funny enough for be a comedy movie and doesn't have enough action (at least realistic or serious) to be an action movie. Or doesn't have a compelling story. Just emotionless killing and forced sad moments that didn't felt natural. Shut your brain and watch it and it might be amusing. So overall it's mediocre.

raw_payload.author: chand-suhas
raw_payload.content:
Nick is a loan shark who is in an unhappy marriage with Alice. But what makes him more unhappy is the fact that his best friend Mike is having an affair with Alice. In the meantime, mob boss Sosa is celebrating the release of his son Jimmy Boy and it's a night of celebration. He vows to eliminate the rat who got Jimmy arrested on the same night. Enters Nick from the future who demands Mike's help in saving Mike and Alice with their main hurdle being Mike from the Present. Why did Nick chose this specific night to return to and will he be able to convince the other Nick to save Mike and Alice, forms rest of the story.

It is always fun when a time machine is thrown into the mix, even when it's about a simple revenge story. BenDavid Grabinski's story is potent enough to establish the three main characters early on while hyping the side characters like Jimmy Boy, Sosa and Tom with enough suspense around the contract killer. It started off on a solid note with enough humor as Vince Vaughn took over as two Nicks while James Marsden easily got into the character of Mick. It was a smooth watch for the initial hour, without going overboard with the time travel and clever use of Gilmore Girls. It is only after this, the narrative went down as the proceedings became quite routine and unengaging. When there was nothing much to explore the three main characters, I wished for more from Jimmy Boy atleast as his character became the reason for the whole revenge angle, considering Nick's angle was resolved quickly. The last hour dragged on with one lengthy action scene while the main antagonist character got portrayed to be too weak. The conveniently written final act couldn't maintain the same level of excitement of the initial hour.

raw_payload.author: PapaLuigis
raw_payload.content: I had so much hope for this! It started out very strong! Action packed and very funny. It also ended pretty strong! But it kinda 💩 the bed throughout the middle. It just kinda fell off, and lost all of it's momentum. I still think it's worth a watch, It was funny in spots, action packed in spots, slow in a lot of spots, but i wouldn't turn anyone away from it.

raw_payload.author: Hakihiko
raw_payload.content:
"Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice" is a light and somewhat entertaining watch that never quite aims higher than its premise allows. It has its moments, and it flows easily enough, but it also gives the feeling of staying comfortably in a middle ground without really standing out.

The performances are one of its stronger aspects. The casting works well, and the chemistry between the characters helps keep the film engaging, even when the material itself is not particularly strong. There is a natural presence that adds a bit of life to the overall experience.

Visually, it keeps things simple and functional. Nothing feels out of place, but nothing really catches the eye either. The soundtrack follows the same line, supporting the scenes without adding much personality.

The script sits in a middle to lower tier, with ideas that feel familiar and not fully developed. The pacing is steady, but it does not build toward anything particularly impactful, making the film feel somewhat flat as it progresses.

Because of this, it can feel a bit forgettable, with moments that pass without leaving a strong impression or emotional weight.

Still, thanks to the solid casting and a few entertaining moments, it remains a decent watch. It may not offer much beyond that, but it manages to be just engaging enough to carry itself through.

raw_payload.author: nightringer-76840
raw_payload.content:
. As an action film, it's just a low budget HK shootout copycat. As a mafia film, it didn't have it. As a comedy, it's not even funny HaHa. As a romantic film, let's not go there. However, as a mix bag film, everthing clicked together and worked great.

Adding to it's strange theme, the actors looked like having great time making this film. Happy actors made the film extra good.

It's a weekend mindless entertainment. Grab a beer and popcorn. Bring your friends of partner. Dive into the chaos and enjoy.

raw_payload.author: amoscow74
raw_payload.content:
I had a good time. It's an uneven mess with shallow characters, a silly story, and a pretty poor script. But it's a fun romp, with some good moments, and an ending that actually hit quite hard.

The relationship between the three main characters is endearing and while there are little to no stakes, you can't help but care about how things will turn out. Could it have been better? Sure. Does it miss more than it hits? Sadly, yes.

And yet, it is definitely worth a watch.

raw_payload.author: ChamindaN
raw_payload.content:
This indie drama explores complicated relationships, identity, and emotional tension between four interconnected characters. The film leans heavily on dialogue and character-driven storytelling rather than action, making it feel intimate and sometimes intense.

The performances are natural and realistic, especially in how they portray confusion, love, and personal struggles. However, the pacing can feel slow at times, and the narrative may seem a bit fragmented if you're expecting a straightforward plot.

Visually, it keeps things simple, focusing more on emotions than spectacle. Overall, it's a thoughtful but niche film-best suited for viewers who enjoy deep, character-focused dramas rather than fast-moving stories.

raw_payload.author: jamminjustin
raw_payload.content: If you want a comedy movie that involves time travel, go watch any Back to the Future movie, even if you've seen them all 100 times. Bc watching any of those movies for the 101st time is still MUCH better than watching this dud of a movie for the 1st time. Zero laughs. Awful writing & plot. Etc. Just not a single good thing I can say about this mess of a "movie". Hollywood A-tier comedies desperately need a return, so badly. Bc I can't take much more of this B-tier "comedy" trash, no matter how much I typically love watching Vince Vaughn, in just about anything.

raw_payload.author: Movieandtvlover
raw_payload.content:
Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is a film about as you might expect Mike, who wants out of organized crime, Future Nick who wants to right his wrongs, Present Nick who helped get them into this mess, and Alice, who is married to Present Nick, but who is also fooling around with Mike behind his back for very understandable reasons. You got your fun antagonists played by Keith David and Jimmy Tatro, but the title characters are the main players this movie focuses on. Essentially this movie is a crime comedy that infuses action and time travel elements that work and how the film plays out is different enough and has a nice style and great soundtrack that it succeeds in giving us all a great time. It's eager to please.

It does play with some cliches and has fun with them, but the movie is fast-paced, funny, and is a total crowd-pleaser. The gimmick is Vince Vaughn playing two roles as the present and future versions of himself and he's someone I like as a performer and he's great here too. It's mainly a comedy, but it features a ton of great action and surprising heart, and the entire cast is clearly having a great time and each adds a nice piece to the film. Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice, premiered in some festivals, but is now on Hulu and I'd encourage you all to forget about your troubles for two hours and just have a blast as too many people take things way too seriously. This one does not.

raw_payload.author: moviemom23
raw_payload.content:
The first scene made me hopeful for a fun, crazy romp with 2 of my favorite actors. Vince Vaughn captured my heart in Wedding Crashers and I still have a soft spot for his fast-talkin' humor. James Marsden is great in everything he's in, and easy on the eyes (wink wink)

Alas it fell apart pretty quickly. The premise isn't terrible, per se, it just doesn't land like it should.

I didn't make it to the end.

raw_payload.author: DUDE-I-DO-REVIEWS
raw_payload.content:
Go into this movie knowing nothing, like I did. It makes it way more fun. It's a solid action crime comedy that just works.

First off, the cast is great. Vince Vaughn is always fantastic with his presence and delivery. Pair him with the totally underrated James Marsden and you've got magic. Then you round things out with Eiza González, Keith David, Jimmy Tatro, Ben Schwartz, and Stephen Root-just to name a few. Oh, and a super sweet cameo. Everyone delivers exactly what's needed.

The story is a nice take on a concept I won't spoil, and I liked the tight "one night only" setup. The party and after-party moments are great, and the whole plot feels unique and fresh.

The comedy lands, and there's some really good action throughout. The final showdown was a highlight for me. I had an awesome time with this, and I loved the way it ended.

When I tell you this could lead to an amazing sequel, and maybe even a full trilogy, it'd be unreal. I'd be all in for that.

So yeah, check out MIKE & NICK & NICK & ALICE. I highly recommend it. You'll have a great time.

There are some slow-motion shots that feel a bit weird and a little misplaced.

raw_payload.author: Billy_Boy_
raw_payload.content:
I know this isn't exactly an unpopular opinion, but - after a promising start - this movie loses a lot of steam in its second half. Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice grabs your attention right from the start. The premise feels fresh: A mix of crime comedy and light sci-fi, with enough edge to keep your interest. The pacing in the first half is quick, and the dialogue and jokes all work relatively well.

The charismatic cast helps as well, particularly because of their nice chemistry. The dialogue and jokes just bounce off each other in a way that feels natural and effortless.

However, as the film hits its midpoint, everything starts to unravel and lose its way. The tone shifts (and not in a good way). The clever, tight and focused film from the first half gives way to a noisy, unfocused mess. The plot turns feel random rather than bold, and scenes either drag or rush by.

The frustrating thing here is seeing what this film could have been. The first half had structure and energy, and also knew what it wanted to be and was comfortable with that.

Instead, by the end we're left with a mediocre film: Uneven, yet not terrible, but still nowhere near what it could have been.

raw_payload.author: orgrabbit
raw_payload.content: Normally I would give any movie with Eiza Gonzalez at least 5 stars. Unfortunately 2 Vince Vaughns dragged it even further down. Vince, yet again, plays Vince. This time however he plays Vince twice. A newly self-aware and regretful high level mobster and the 6 months younger vengeful mobster he has evolved from. The whole premise of redemption and growth falls flat in this ill-conceived genre blender. It's as if this was the first draft of the script and they ran out of money for rewrites, reshoots, directing, and scoring. This is another straight to streaming flop that will make you shiver at the thought of a teased possible sequel. If you like a brief bit of action, comedy, violence, heart, and revenge then this movie isn't for you, it tried. If you like Eiza Gonzalez or even James Marsden then this movie has them both, and that's about it.

raw_payload.author: Peace_Fog
raw_payload.content:
Solid movie. Easy to understand time travel plot

The story is a basic revenge plot and while it is simple, the cast has excellent charisma. Very enjoyable action flick. Great use of blocking so you know what's going on with the action. They really frame the whole scene, then focus on close ups. So you don't get lost in the action

The plot is simple and easy to follow. Characters are memorable and hilarious. It's not trying to say something, but that's fine. Not every movie needs to be Citizen Kane. The dialogue is quick and you can tell all the actors are really enjoying themselves

My only real gripe is the slow motion scenes obviously used the same camera as the rest of the movie so there's a noticeable stutter as the frames are halved. It's not enough to make me stop watching the film but it is noticeable

Solid action movie.

raw_payload.author: Brina223
raw_payload.content:
In a cinematic landscape where it feels like every second release is more concerned with delivering a lecture on contemporary social sensibilities than actually telling a story, Mike and Nick And Nick And Alice is a welcome relief. It is a genuine breath of fresh air that prioritises entertainment over the usual prescriptive moralising that has captured the industry. Instead of being bogged down by the tedious, half-hour character introductions that usually waste everyone's time, this film gets straight to the heart of the matter. The pacing is relentless in the best way possible, ensuring you are never left waiting for the actual plot to kick in.

It is a rare bit of fun that keeps things tight and avoids the glaring narrative gaps that often plague modern blockbusters. It's a really good thing about this thing that it doesn't take itself too seriously. It knows its purpose is simply to offer enjoyment. Those who walk into a cinema expecting a guided tour through the latest blueprints for social engineering will likely be disappointed. However, for anyone who just wants a fast-paced, engaging ride that doesn't try to play schoolmaster, this is a fantastic choice. It delivers exactly what it promises without any of the exhausting baggage that usually comes with modern cinema.

raw_payload.author: SoumikBanerjee1996
raw_payload.content:
Yes, it's fun and engaging in parts, especially when the intensity dial is turned way up or the comedic punches seem to land; still, I believe there isn't enough 'bite' and 'finesse' to create an action thriller with a not-so-original science fiction spin.

One of those movies where your opinion will be heavily influenced by the expectations you have set for the film as well as for all the main characters in the story. If you enjoy the actors and whatever they pull off on screen, you will have a much better time than me!

raw_payload.author: uberdonkey6
raw_payload.content:
So mobster goes back in time to save his friend's life who the earlier him stitched up. I hate depressing gritty mobster films, and thankfully this isn't one of them. There are some nice jokes playing of the fact of the guy going back in time meeting himself and not necessarily having the same opinion. In terms of humour, it's not really a laugh fest, but neither are the jokes slapstick or cheesy, so what it comes across as is light hearted and fun.

I really liked how the three leads played off each other, and yes Eiza is great and also beautiful, but we should also give credit to the two other established actors. Dialogue is good, filming nice, and most importantly it is an interesting and fun story.

The heart of the film I guess is about friendship, which isn't overplayed, but makes it a sweet and fun film. I also liked the other characters in the film, especially the more dim witted one that went to prison (his release being around which the story revolves). Neither too simple nor too complex (which is surprising for modern time travel movies!). I also liked the soundtrack in this film and thought was built nicely into it.

While this isn't a film that will go down in history, it's better than just a Sunday afternoon flick, and I think the writers and director as well as the actors can be proud that it is an interesting, enjoyable and fun film. I recommend it for most people that aren't looking for anything heavy. It isn't gory or very violent but there are a few scenes where people get shot. Overall, I highly recommend.

raw_payload.author: trailerfreak
raw_payload.content: Ok, you know this is implausible once you know what's going on, but just go with it. Yes, it could have been tighter and not so long. But it achieved its goal of entertaining for me. Silly, fun and entertaining. Not as bad ir a waste of time as others have expressed. Wouldn't we all like to fix something that we have regret.

raw_payload.author: FiftyTwo_52
raw_payload.content:
M & N & N & A starts as a razor-sharp, genre-blending romp (think Looper meets Grosse Pointe Blank) with Vince Vaughn's double act and James Marsden's roguish charm delivering 8/10-worthy thrills. The first half crackles with wit, chaotic energy, and genuinely clever time-travel twists, making it a standout for fans of dark, sci-fi humour.

Then the second half hits. What begins as a promising mashup devolves into a baffling, tonally inconsistent mess. The randomness feels less like creative risk-taking and more like a script that lost its way, squandering the momentum and leaving you wondering what the hell just happened.

It's a shame, because the cast's chemistry and the premise deserved better.

5.5/10: a film of two halves, where the first is a blast and the second is a head-scratching letdown. If only they'd stuck the landing.

raw_payload.author: Grahamscorner1994
raw_payload.content:
Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is a movie that clearly aims to be different. It mixes a crime story with comedy and throws in a time-travel element that turns a simple night into something much more chaotic. The concept is interesting and definitely caught my attention going in.

One of the biggest positives is the cast. Vince Vaughn playing two versions of Nick is easily the standout part of the film. He brings a lot of energy and helps keep the movie entertaining even when the story starts to wander. The chemistry between the characters works well at times, and there are a few genuinely funny moments that land.

However, the movie struggles with tone. It shifts quickly between comedy, action, and more serious moments, which makes the overall flow feel uneven. The pacing in the middle slows down quite a bit, and the plot becomes a little confusing with all the twists and time-travel elements. Some jokes feel forced, and a few scenes could have been trimmed.

Overall, it's not a bad watch, but it feels like a movie that had a great idea and didn't fully execute it as well as it could have. There's still some fun to be had, especially if you enjoy unusual, genre-blending films.

raw_payload.author: vencken
raw_payload.content: Vince and James compete for a naivety cup. Already enough to keep it going. Add some cringy humor, a wandering story line and Dolph Lundgrens brains being used for new wall decor and I just could not stop watching. Actually kept on coming back after interruptions. Also nice camera work and quality FX in action scenes made it a nice one to watch.

raw_payload.author: jah9000
raw_payload.content:
A mistake happens on Monday.

On Wednesday, the person who made the mistake travels back in time to Monday to prevent himself from making the mistake.

In the attempt, that person kills his past self.

And so, a paradox is created.

The person does not survive to Wednesday in order to travel back in time. Therefore, none of his efforts actually occur.

And so, no matter what BenDavid Grabinski tries, this movie still exists.

raw_payload.author: dharmacrown-83684
raw_payload.content: The first half of the movie was surprisingly thrilling and entertaining, great dialogues, acting and direction. Vince Vaughn was funny and the whole time travel story was compelling. Then somewhere halfway through the writers for some reason decided it's time to shift to dumb action mode and the brilliance of the first half remained history. Shame, this had the potential of being a great movie but ended up poorly.

raw_payload.author: wattsau-07974
raw_payload.content:
Did not have any expectations about this film i never liked Vince Vaughn movies , But this one was Actually GOOD been a while when it surprise you at the end wanting more it was action and entertaining fun .

Nice, fun, light, self aware. The plot is full of holes and it doesn't care. Hell, it's almost its selling point.

The actors are good (what else could you expect from a Vaughn and Mardsen duo ?). Action is great, jokes are just dumb enough to make you laugh.

It's a good silly movie.

raw_payload.author: Aspekt
raw_payload.content:
Went in to the movie blind, had no clue that Vince Vaughn even did movies anymore until I saw this show up in my feed.

The movie starts weird for the first couple of minutes but you kind of get a sense to what's going to happen as it unfolds. There are some great moments, some funny moments and surprisingly decent action moments.

It's not a typical comedy, it's a darker kind of comedy with some heavy action sequences. Vince still shines while he technically riffs twice as long since there's two of him.

Enjoy the film instead of trying to scrutinize the whole thing. It's a fun movie, I haven't seen a decent comedy or action movie in a long time so I'm excited.

raw_payload.author: AshleeG-17
raw_payload.content: I dont comment let alone review, I mostly only do this when I'm dissapointed. However, this movie gave all the feels, nostalgia and reminiscing of child hood and present feelings, music was an absulute hit in many ways. The comedy and jokes even the grey scale old memories was a nice touch. This is how movies should be made feelings, intentions, love and emotion. This movie is a 10 of 10 and that's coming from a hard critic like I said I don't comment ever and this movie was amazing.

raw_payload.author: patosaloo
raw_payload.content:
It's a movie to relax, turn your brain off (exactly what I needed) and I laughed a LOT! Vince, James and Eiza are spectacular!

This is not a movie you watch to analyze or overthink. It's a movie you watch to enjoy. It's silly, a little absurd, and honestly makes no real sense at times but that's exactly where its charm lives.

The humor is playful and unapologetically dumb in the best way possible.

It's the kind of film where you stop asking "why?" and just go along for the ride. And by the end, you realize you had a really good time doing exactly that.

A well-made, nonsensical, feel-good comedy that reminds you not every movie needs to be serious to be worth watching.

raw_payload.author: poulos69
raw_payload.content:
The trailer was better than the film. Was looking forward to seeing this and then about a third of the way in it got dumb. Then it only got worse after that. Don't waste your time.

I like the actors and thw concept but the script and the directing were so bad, I don't know why the cast agreed to do this.

raw_payload.author: mwsmbngsimon
raw_payload.content:
I gave it a 4, because it's tolerable enough for a person to finish! But what a mess? Within minutes, it becomes clear the film is aiming to be a comedy But where are the jokes? Dialogues are badly written so none of the punchline lands except for a few chuckles

Remember action comedies that are actually funny like "Spy 2015" featuring Jason Statham, well this is not it!

The only way to justify any kind of rating is to approach it strictly as a comedy; viewed from any other angle, it's simply a failure.

raw_payload.author: ziad-burhani
raw_payload.content:
Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice was a surprisingly enjoyable watch. The film leans more into atmosphere and character-driven moments rather than a typical straightforward plot, which gives it a unique and fresh feel.

Emily Browning delivers a strong performance, bringing depth and emotion to the story, while Vince Vaughn adds his usual charisma in a more toned-down, grounded role. The supporting cast, including Giovanni Ribisi, also does a solid job keeping the film engaging.

Visually, the movie stands out with its stylish direction and moody tone. Even when the pacing slows, it still holds your attention and builds an interesting vibe throughout.

It's not perfect, but that's part of its charm. Overall, a good and slightly different film.

raw_payload.author: arungeorge13
raw_payload.content:
This was honestly a BLAST for a March straight-to-streaming entry, I mean. The entire cast is firing on all cylinders, the script has plenty of odd gags, and the action is surprisingly solid. Vince Vaughn plays the two Nicks (in the title), while James Marsden and Eiza Gonzalez play Mike and Alice, respectively. Seriously, the logline on IMDB is probably the weirdest way to describe the plot -- it sounds like a basic gangster film. LB definitely does it better, yet what it doesn't tell you is how FUN the whole thing is packaged together.

The plot setup offsets the age difference among Vaughn, Marsden, and Gonzalez, and once it gets going, the pace only dips during flashback montages and extended scenes. Yeah, the leads aren't written in the most likeable (or relatable) ways, but what binds them together are (firstly) the unique plot circumstances and (secondly) the dynamic they share. The film also benefits from a slew of great supporting acts from the likes of Keith David, Jimmy Tatro, Dolph Lundgren, Lewis Tan, Arturo Castro, and Ben Schwartz. Tatro, especially, is absolutely BONKERS. One or two needledrops (like the Dave Matthews track in a strip club) felt slightly OFF, but the rest of the soundtrack (ft. Oasis, Papa Roach, The Chemical Brothers, and more) is amazing.

Except for the Gilmore Girls lore that everyone in the film kept discussing about, I enjoyed the pop-culture references that contributed heavily to the humour quotient. The "step-printing" effect was used on more than one occasion, lessening its impact, though Larry Fong's cinematography is certainly among the positives when viewed holistically. I'm not familiar with writer-director BenDavid Grabinski's previous works, so this counts as a more-than-decent FIRST for me. There are some MISSES, but the HITS are worth your while in this exciting genre-mash.

raw_payload.author: saolivaresm
raw_payload.content:
In Hulu/Disney+'s new showcase, BenDavid Grabinski presents an action comedy that blends time travel with the gangster genre. Vince Vaughn, in a dual role, drives a plot that relies more on the chemistry of its cast-James Marsden and Eiza González complete the triangle-than on the originality of its script.

The film works as immediate entertainment: brisk pacing, effective humor during moments of identity confusion, and a cast that knows how to hold your attention. However, viewed from the perspective of a film blog or specialized magazine, it comes across as a catalog product, designed to be quickly consumed on the platform and forgotten just as easily.

Between formula and fleeting charm

Grabinski avoids tedium with dynamic direction, but the story quickly becomes tiresome. The interplay of stunt doubles and villains adds a spark, though it never transcends mere gags. Compared to other action comedies on Netflix (Red Notice, The Out-Laws) or Prime Video (Shotgun Wedding, My Spy), this offering feels more modest, less spectacular, and less franchise-oriented.

The franchise dilemma

The film hints at continuity, but this depends on how much the viewer is willing to accept a story that recycles familiar formulas. While it has some successful comedic moments and well-handled touches of drama, it fails to forge a unique identity that sets it apart in the saturated streaming market.

Conclusion

Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is a decent way to pass the time, a title that delivers on its promise of entertaining the family on a weekend, but one that will hardly be remembered as anything more than "another platform action comedy." Its charm lies in the cast and the pacing, but its lack of narrative risk condemns it to being a product of instant consumption, closer to the algorithm than to cinephilia.

raw_payload.author: masonsaul
raw_payload.content:
Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice is a great high concept action comedy with ample heart and humour. It uses the time travel premise to allow one (but technically two) of its main characters to atone for their biggest mistake by trying to get their former self to be a better person. The core four are strong enough to sustain the film in between the really good action sequences and the jokes are really funny, allowing all of its memorable cast of characters to get a moment to shine.

Everyone here is fantastic. Vince Vaughn excels as both versions of Nick, ensuring the one from the future is warmer and full of regret compared to the present day version who is colder with a lot more room for growth over the course of this journey. James Marsden is having so much fun as the person who is the most confused by time travelling. The chemistry he has with Eiza González is given the necessary time to feel believable and she gets to be part of everything too.

Even though there are some creative choices that don't always work, BenDavid Grabinski's direction gets so much right and has plenty of style thanks to Larry Fong's cinematography delivering loads of slick shots so it never stops being a good time. His breezy yet heartfelt writing keeps the time travel straightforward and has a few fun surprises alongside a very consistent gag rate. The soundtrack also adds greater personality since most of the needle drops are used to make the action more fun.

raw_payload.author: danchy-jug
raw_payload.content: The film is not boring, there are funny scenes and it is entertaining. The music from the film is reminiscent of the music from some older action films, and that's well-known songs. It takes me back to when movies and music were great. And not what they shoot today and what they give Oscars for is not normal. I rated it a 10 because it took me back in time with that music to memories when the film industry was better.

raw_payload.author: SnoopyStyle
raw_payload.content:
Alice (Eiza González) is married to loan shark Nick (Vince Vaughn), but she's having an affair with his gun thug underling Mike (James Marsden). Nick gets Mike to do a job. It's a big surprise when the job turns out to be another version of Nick.

There is some good time-loopy fun here. I like Marsden. I like Vaughn although I don't care if he survives. That's probably the missing ingredient. I need to root for his survival. This has a bit of off-kilter action song montage fun and that's a fine popcorn watch.

raw_payload.author: alatlantic
raw_payload.content:
The nicest thing I can say for this movie is that it's a decently fun watch, if you turn your brain off and just veg out.

The story is complete nonsense, but it's not really the point here as I suspect it was meant to be silly. The problem there is that as a silly movie, it just isn't that funny. There are a couple groaners and such, but not much else.

I love Vaughn, but he's looking as old and uninterested as I've ever seen him, and I can't say I blame him.

The premise of the movie isn't so bad, but everything else about it lets that premise down.

raw_payload.author: ChrisD-192
raw_payload.content:
Ok. I laughed at the Rory' boyfriend scene. Vince is interesting in multiple parts. The premise is pretty silly but it is just plain entertainment. No more. No less. Don't go in expecting Platoon or dr zhivago. Its just a bit of a laugh. Oh yeah. Dean was a pretty bad boyfriend for Rory.

Also. Avoid falling for the finger guns dance. It never works.

raw_payload.author: maxborunov
raw_payload.content: I liked the cast in this movie it also had an engaging plot and absurdity throughout the film. The silliness keeps the entertsinment going. I liked the charecters and plot their stories and over the top acting keep the story engaging and interesting along with the action sequences. This movie to me feels like it relys on profanity for its jokes despite the absurd actions that do happen which isnt enough to be close to hilarious. It could of had more action, or more sources to humor, or better charecter development near the end. Other wise this was an overall entertaining film with an excellent cast with enjoyable performances. Good but not great. This is why i give this film a 7/10.

raw_payload.author: Jonathan-4805
raw_payload.content: The opening credits sum this film up. If you like the opening credits, then this film is for you. If, like me, you cringe, then don't continue. The film is inconsistent in tone, with many boring filler sequences, jokes which just do not land, and a lot of violence, particularly at the end, which just makes no sense. (I specifically signed up to IMDB to give this review because I was so annoyed at how bad this film is and how nevertheless it seems to have achieved a rating of above 6!)

raw_payload.author: Hughmyerz
raw_payload.content: Loved Vince Vaughn James Marsden and Eiza Gonzales they were great loved the acting form them can't say the rest for the entire cast some of the other actors weren't so great but the story was entertaining and thrilling definitely and bit of a mind twister but it was funny offbeat loved the action sequences and the ending overall I'd rate it a 6.6 out of 10.

raw_payload.author: linlarrabee
raw_payload.content:
It was one of those movies sounded fun and you never know. It was surprisingly funny. I liked it a lot. The actors were great. Loved Vince Vaughn James Martin.. there characters and chemistry. The Best!

Gina spot was was right on cue. His facial expressions his commentary. He was just absolutely great. Really made the movie. Even when they were fighting, I generally find that too long in most movies. The music in the background the way they played it out. I enjoyed all the fights. Surprising for me. Was interesting.

raw_payload.author: angelontheisland
raw_payload.content: Say what you want - it was nice to just be entertained. It has been awhile since I was not put off by an H. R. Lesson mid scene. This one kept me interested from beginning to end because the whole cast was pure talent. Fun and not taking itself or anything too seriously - whilst respecting the viewer. Just a great ensemble. If you go in a Vince fan and simply wanting to be entertained for a short while - you will enjoy yourself. I sure did. More like these please.

raw_payload.author: juliehampton2012
raw_payload.content:
Loved it. Clever, visually stunning, and full of thoughtful details-from the set design to the architecture, everything felt beautifully done.

The Gilmore Girls references were such a fun touch, and the cat character completely stole the show.

It had a great mix of excitement, action, and humor that kept it engaging the whole way through.

Really hoping this gets a sequel!

raw_payload.author: Dannyms14
raw_payload.content:
So basically, the movie has a normal script, basic jokes with repetitive punchlines, that could've been overviewed if the director wasn't so obsessed with having slow shutter effect in the majority of the frames, the soundtrack doesn't go with the moments of humour whatsoever, and you cannot fully sympathise with the main characters. Seems also that the director also loves the 90's and early 2000's movies since he tried to imitate some of the styles of those in fashion at that time. Nevertheless, critics aside if you like something to put in the background while cleaning the house this is perfect.

This movie could've been saved and being decent if he didn't do all of that editing to the movie since the acting is good and the actors made a good job.

raw_payload.author: kuner-59029
raw_payload.content:
Vince Vaughn. He seems like a good guy, and he's a versatile actor who excels at comedy and drama. I hadn't seen him in anything since Dragged Across Concrete, and went into this film blind, not knowing what to expect.

It's... uneven. How even to describe this? It's a pulpy crime comedy with a time travel angle that switches between wannabe Robert Rodriguez to wannabe ultraviolent John Wick to buddy comedy or love triangle comedy. The movie never settles into any tone and constantly changes genres.

This may sound bad, but the writing oftentimes makes up for it. Some of the dialogue is extremely well written. The villains are all entertaining to watch - until they become too dumb for their down good.

All villains have fun names straight out of a 90s Ninja Turtles style video game. Quickdraw Mike, Napalm something, Roid Rage Ryan Randy, Dumbass Tony. All the gangsters (exept Keith David) are written extremely stupid beyond parody. Sometimes this works, othertimes it goes too far, but most of the time it's entertaining.

The scene at the gas station with the stoner attendant is an absolute highlight that shows Vince Vaughn at his best. The young guy playing the stoner is brilliant, too, as is the lady cop. That moment alone is worth watching the movie.

That's not to say the rest is bad. The story's structure is actually quite brilliant. No glaring logic flaws, decent character motivations... it all works, right up until the moment the hitman shows up. After that, the film falls apart. The pacing is broken up by tedious flashbacks detailing how the love triangle happened, explaining who knew what, when we as the audience already knew. For 10 minutes, the narrative grinds to a halt. After that, we get a pointless John Wick style shootout that's extremely poorly executed. The music switches constantly, going through various 90s songs and even playing the blood shower track from 1998's Blade and the full Reptile fight music from the 1996 Mortal Kombat movie. It doesn't fit the tone of this film, it instantly feels out of place, calling back to a better film, and it doesn't even match the choreography. That entire ending shootout shouldn't have happened. The film was too clever up until that point to have it just devolve like that. The ultraviolence felt out of place, and Vince Vaughn is clearly too old to be doing these kinds of action scenes.

Also the film wastes Dolph Lundgren's appearance.

Vaugh and Marsden work well together. The female lead wasn't great. She wore the same facial expression and tone the entire movie. Whenever she's on screen, the film begins to drag. She's the typical Disneyfied female character and seemingly emulates the Olga Korylenko style of acting. Flat. No flaws, nothing interesting. For those interested, the recent "Send Help" is a great example of how to write and perform a female lead charcter in a dark comedy.

Despite all these flaws, I still give the film a generous 7 stars. Why? Solid script, good performances by the male leads, fun world building, a brisk pace and the gas station scene. If one were to cut out some of the dumber scenes like the flashbacks or exposition dumps, it could have been a solid 8.

raw_payload.author: Eljefedejeffy
raw_payload.content: I thought it was a good movie. Nothing groundbreaking or epic, but definitely worth a watch and a lot of fun. The cast was actually really good. I liked all of the cast members. I wonder if there will be a sequel. One of the better movies for Vince Vaughn as of lately. If you don't expect much or want something fun to watch you won't be let down.

raw_payload.author: rosebudsk
raw_payload.content: This is not Oscar material, this was a fun, silly movie. Kind of a spoof on some of the 80s action movies. Excellent sound track. Gave it an 8 because it's not a 6!!! I had gotten kind of annoyed with the typical Vince Vaughan a-hole characters. He's still one of those, but in a way that tells you he's in on the joke.

raw_payload.author: Canonhead
raw_payload.content:
Action, love, violence, and just the right amount of stupidity-that's the best way to describe this one.

I went in with zero expectations and honestly had no idea what it was about, and I'm glad I kept it that way. Not knowing anything made the experience better, so I won't spoil it for you either. Just trust me on this-go in blind if you can. Thanks.

This is a straight-up popcorn movie. The kind you throw on when you don't want to think too hard, don't want a deep message, and definitely aren't trying to learn anything. It's just there to entertain you for a couple of hours, and it does that job really well. There's a good mix of action, some surprisingly decent love elements, a little bit of chaos, and plenty of moments where you'll laugh at how ridiculous things get.

Is it groundbreaking? Not even close. Is it going to win awards? Probably not. But that's not the point. Sometimes you just want a movie that knows exactly what it is and doesn't try to be anything more. This one leans into the fun, the nonsense, and the over-the-top moments, and that's what makes it work.

If you're looking for something serious, this isn't it. But if you just want to relax, laugh, and enjoy a wild ride without overthinking anything, this is definitely worth the watch.

raw_payload.author: T2TLED
raw_payload.content:
I was against this movie at first, but it quickly won me over. What a great concept for a movie. The comedy was great. The soundtrack was nostalgic. And the nods and references to a certain The WB show was hysterical. Ignore the haters and enjoy the ride. It's a fun buddy comedy movie mixed in with romance, action and more.

Just sit back and let it roll.

raw_payload.author: destroyerwod
raw_payload.content:
Everything that gravitate around Eiza Gonzalez (in a gorgeous dress to say the least), James Marsden, Vince Vaughn and well...Vince Vaughn, all of that worked well for me. The comedy is there, its funny, the back and forth between the same actor playing 2 roles is well done and obviously due to technology these days, it can be done effortlessly.

Where i had a bit of an issue was basically the rest. I understand they wanted to go for a comedy and not a serious action movie, but i felt the vilains were so shallow and so little is developed about this "organization" itself. Characters seem so over the top that it get a bit ridiculous and yet at the same time they had some "known actors" in certain roles that were completely wasted. While one of them was obviously just a cameo and that is ok, another one i felt they could had done a lot more with.

The time travel aspect and machine is also underdeveloped and only serve as a conductor so you can have a movie. While sometimes you don't need explanations on how (and why) it work, Hot Tub Time Machine coming to mind, i felt in this case a little more would had been welcomed.

That said, the movie is still entertaining. It work because the main actors are likeable, their dialogues are funny, and it feels like you not suppose to think too hard about it.

As a direct to streaming movie it absolutely fulfill its role. I would say its a solid 7 out of 10, but miss the bump to an 8. Once again i wish i could rate it 7.5 because this is exactly where it fit with me.

raw_payload.author: gilesalecgoodacre
raw_payload.content:
This is utter garbage. I do laugh when I see so called critics giving this a 7/10. Seriously who employs these people, they have no idea what they're talking about.

The film is unfunny, boring, a copy of many previous films combined and quite frankly poorly acted.

Don't be fooled by critics, this film is not worth seeing nor paying for. Like most films from the past decade or two.

raw_payload.author: actual_lee
raw_payload.content: I enjoyed it more than I thought I would. What stood out to me was the acting, dialogue, editing, music and of course the script which really made this movie fun to watch. It succeeds in a new and fresh take on time travel even though it is a topic that can feel over done in modern film making. While it's the core of the story it doesn't dwell too much on it, you just get sucked into the story and run with it. Recommend this movie if you're in the mood for fast paced action flick with a comedy aspect. It ticks all the boxes.

raw_payload.author: Skepticos-Realitis
raw_payload.content:
As much as I can appreciate the idea of thoughtful and reformed gangsters and hitmen, partners that learn to accept that their spouses are not chattel and that when a relatiionship is over, both parties as individual human beings should maturely hang up their ego's and move on with their lives, or, that many underworld figures are in fact half-wits rather than street-wise geniuses, 'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice' really doesn't do it for me. Part gangsta film, part "comedy", part sci-fi, part romance and melodrama BenDavid Grabinski(who) 's film doesn't really nail any of these genre's.

Whilst I suspect it is mostly aimed at the "Comedy" element, it just doesn't have the timing to pull this off, and whilst the cast do a fairly good imitation of a walk through, they themselves seem a little underwhelmed by the material. Quicken the pace, lose the flashbacks, give the nick(sic)named characters actual eccentricities to hang the names on, and supply a cockney voice-over, and you might have something...There are loose hints of a sequel, but for god's sake, say it ain't so...

raw_payload.author: AucesG
raw_payload.content:
I forced myself to sit through the first 35 minutes, hoping things would pick up or at least become somewhat engaging, but that never happened. It only got more tedious as it went on. Eventually, I decided I'd had enough, turned it off, and walked away, convinced it was complete garbage overall.

Not worth the time.

raw_payload.author: W_L_Campbell
raw_payload.content:
When a mob boss's son is released from prison, he makes a vow to find and eliminate the snitch that got his boy sent away. Unfortunately for that mob boss, another member of his cadre has other plans and will go to extreme lengths to protect the alleged snitch.

This is not a perfect film. It's not a great film. But it is HIGHLY entertaining.

Vince Vaughn is at his smartass best, and James Marsden has developed his chops well enough to stand alongside him. The ageless Eiza González brings a gravitas to a film that is ultimately rather unserious, balancing the thriller aspect of this fun action comedy. Featuring special appearances by Keith David, Stephen Root, Dolph Lundgren, Emily Hampshire, and Dylan Playfair, this is an hour and forty minutes of laughter and recoils that make you say "Ooh!"

raw_payload.author: gibbsgregory
raw_payload.content: I loved it. But need a sequel!! A Mike & Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice movie! This it would be entertaining as hell! The action was fantastic and seeing James Marsden pulling off all the Jason Borne style action sequences is brilliant! Loved the gun fights and then having Dolph Lundgren as a cannibal was brilliant! He looked so terrifying! I want to see all of them again in a sequel but I think they should really get the scientist, special effects guy and the baron more screen time too!

raw_payload.author: daisukereds
raw_payload.content:
Time-loop plot with gangsters and romance.

As far as time machine usages, this is okay. It does feel like an excuse for things to happen, and I wonder if it was really necessary.. thankfully, the premise is as fresh as it could be and it effectively uses it in a way that is central to the story and to keep things moving. The film is almost 2 hours but has good pacing, decent dialogue, great action.. and a very annoying stylistic choice of using "5-frames per second" scenes.. Totally unnecessary.

I personally don't like Vince Vaughn and his grandstanding-like persona, but he is ultimately fine.. though he is given a pivotal role that isn't sold on the emotional side. With him, there's always a levity that makes things feel like a joke. Thankfully, there's not a single serious bone in this script's structure, which makes it a fun watch and work just fine.

Now, I do REALLY like Eiza González and James Marsden! And seeing their charming selves interacting in a delightful manner filled me with joy, as their characters are great together. The rest of the "secondary" cast is energetic and memorable, which is a surprise.

I would easily recommend it, and might even rewatch it myself.

raw_payload.author: jared-25331
raw_payload.content:
Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice (2026) is a science fiction action comedy movie and it follows two friends as they navigate through the dangerous world of organized crime, testing their loyalty and survival skills as they get deeper into the criminal underworld. This movie actually first premiered at SXSW, so I decided to give it a watch and I loved it.

Positives for Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice (2026): The story is cliched with a number of plot points you've seen in other movies, but luckily this movie takes those tropes and makes something fun to watch. The execution of the story is handled very well and I was excited to see how the story plays out. This is one of those movies where I didn't predict anything that was going to happen at the end and that rarely happens for me. You are a great ensemble cast led by Vince Vaughn as Nick, James Marsden as Quick Draw Mike and Eiza Gonzalez as Alice, who have fantastic chemistry as this team of main characters and their interactions are so fun and funny to watch. The supporting cast is also awesome and you have Keith David, Jimmy Tatro, Emily Hampshire, Arturo Castro, Lewis Tan, Ben Schwartz, Stephen Root and Dolph Lundgren and it's quite insane how this movie got these people together and they are all awesome. There is a lot of comedy with the dialogue and interactions that are so good as these actors are delivering their lines with each other. There are some great action sequences that had me smiling with the insanity on display. And finally, the ending of this movie was quite sad with what happens to the characters and I wasn't expecting that from this movie and I mean that in a good way.

Negatives for Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice (2026): The movie has some pacing issue that is sprinkled in spots and it does drag the story out, when it could've been tightened with a shorter runtime.

Overall, Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice (2026) is a damn good and entertaining science fiction action comedy movie that I wasn't expecting much out of, but I'm happy I got that with this movie.

raw_payload.author: Closeone
raw_payload.content:
Nice, fun, light, self aware. The plot is full of holes and it doesn't care. Hell, it's almost its selling point.

The characters are fun, lovable. The actors are good (what else could you expect from a Vaughn/Mardsen duo ?). Action is great, jokes are just dumb enough to make you laugh.

It's a great dumb movie.

raw_payload.author: tsvetan_mehochev
raw_payload.content:
This has got to be one of dumbest if not the dumbest movie i have seen in recent memory.

I mean no disrespect and i don't want to be overly rude , but what did the writers took to even come up with such nonsense . I mean ,how dumb can you be or not have care in the world to even finance such a disaster.... I understand and adore humor in general and scandalous jokes , but everything was bland beyond measure ! Don't get me started on the acting . Soo a time machine eh ?! Two Vince Vaughns ?!?! Take a hike . Down the drain you go yet again Hollywood , Wooo hooo ........

raw_payload.author: attilalengyel-78684
raw_payload.content: This is a kind of movie I like so much when I want to relax, and don't take anything seriously. It is a light-hearted crime comedy with a nice twist, nothing you can't digest during its lifespan (90 minutes). There are some really funny moments, mostly delivered by unexpected situations and good acting. All the players are in line here and go with the flow. Good directing, simple but effective camerawork, excellent editing, and smart music choices make this movie a memorable flick. My favourite points are: no shaky camera, no overhyped 'original score', no overacting. 7/10 Recommended > for a nice, relaxed evening.

raw_payload.author: diemarxens
raw_payload.content:
Don´t take it too serious as obvious the movie also doesn´t. Good chemistry between the characters, Vince Vaughn isn´t as annoying as usual, James Mardsen is good, Eiza Gonzalez also even if underused and the rest is there to fill the screen. Good effects, good music choice, some nice camera work and overall a feel-good-movie (in a sort of way).

Grab some popcorn, shut down the logic department and have a good time. Also the runtime is ok.

raw_payload.author: davidbleavitt
raw_payload.content:
So, Mike and Nick and Nick and Alice.

A mostly enjoyed movie.

James Marsden and Vince Vaughn, along with Keith David seemed to have fun with this.

Bit of pot-pourri of other works ranging from Back To The Future, Matrix(visual homage and very well done at that), and other such movies.

On the whole worth a look.

raw_payload.author: reefpye
raw_payload.content: I was pleasantly surprised with this movie. A delightful twist on a classic time travel idea. Vince Vaughn is excellent in this and you really feel for his character by the end. I would strongly recommend you grab some popcorn on a Friday night and sit down and have a watch. I won't be surprised if they make a sequel of this. They could really have some fun with it. Also the music is top notch, classic '90s and '80s as well.
